page: Amazon Web Services r8i-flex.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor operating at 3.9 GHz. The system provides 64 virtual CPUs across 32 physical cores with 2 threads per core, backed by 512.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz. This configuration yields 8.0 GB of RAM per core. There is no local storage or GPU hardware bundled with this instance, and network connectivity is rated at a 15 Gbps baseline.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core processing and memory bandwidth capabilities. The instance achieves top-tier performance in Geekbench single-core tests, software compilation with Clang, image processing, and text processing. Passmark CPU scores and static web serving throughput also rank in the top tier. Multi-core stress-ng tests, bzip3 decompression, and Redis SET operations demonstrate strong performance, while large-block memory write bandwidth and single-core stress-ng performance sit in the average tier.

The Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). These hardware features contribute to strong LLM inference speeds, particularly for prompt processing on small and medium models, which register in the top tier and strong tier respectively. However, text generation on larger 70B models drops to average performance. The instance is suited for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services r8i-flex.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance utilizing the Nitro hypervisor and an x86_64 Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor. It features 64 vCPUs (32 physical cores) running at 3.9 GHz, paired with 512.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a 15 Gbps baseline network connection. Benchmark results show top-tier performance in software compilation, text processing, and static web serving, alongside strong multi-core CPU and memory read bandwidth. The inclusion of Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) enables strong performance in LLM prompt processing and inference. This instance is suited for memory-intensive database operations, enterprise web serving, and CPU-based machine learning workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8i-flex.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier performance in compilation and database operations.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8i-flex.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 platform featuring 512 GB of DDR5 RAM and Intel Xeon processors for data-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor at 3.9 GHz
512.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Top-tier performance in software compilation, text processing, and static web serving
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated CPU-based LLM inference
15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU resources
Suited for large-scale databases, memory-intensive applications, and enterprise web serving
